mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% triggers a labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 7.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 850,000 units signals a surge in demand for construction materials and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 850,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 95 signals a supply-side shock that reduces economic growth and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 95 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that boosts discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial jobless claims below 200,000 triggers a labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 13,500 signals a surge in demand for goods and services that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 13,500 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the real GDP above 13,500 and a decline in industrial production below 95 signals a supply-side shock that reduces economic growth and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 13,500 and INDPRO < 95 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
